title: xyris subulata
intro: Xyris subulata, also known as the yellow-eyed grass or grass-leaved xyris, is a perennial plant species native to Australia, known for its narrow leaves and yellow flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Xyris subulata include its potential to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its antiseptic properties, which can aid in wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
